metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Alice's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Alice swept them up carefully.

Dark clouds gathered above the town during the late afternoon. Heavy rain fell onto the pavement outside the small bookshop. Miriam opened the black umbrella at the bookshop entrance. Miriam walked home along the wet street without getting soaked.
The baker mixed the flour and the water in a bowl. The dough rested near the warm oven for one hour. The dough doubled in size inside the covered bowl. The baker placed the dough into the hot oven.

The old car made a loud noise on the highway. The engine stopped completely beside a quiet country road. Daniel pushed the old car onto the gravel shoulder. Daniel called a mechanic from the nearest petrol station.

The gardener left the wooden gate open by mistake. The small dog ran through the wooden gate into the street. The neighbour caught the small dog near the corner shop. The gardener carried the small dog back into the garden.

The waiter carried the tray across the crowded restaurant floor. The tray tilted sharply beside a narrow wooden table. The hot coffee spilled onto the customer's white shirt. The waiter brought a clean towel and a sincere apology.

The strong wind shook the old oak beside the farmhouse. The old oak cracked loudly near the base of the trunk. The old oak fell across the narrow farm road. The farmer cut the old oak into short logs.

Timo carried the red kite to the open field. The red kite rose quickly on the steady summer wind. The red kite caught the branches of the tall poplar. Timo climbed the tall poplar and freed the red kite.

Nora lit the tall candle on the dinner table. The tall candle burned steadily throughout the long evening meal. The tall candle shrank to a small stump of wax. Nora blew out the tall candle before midnight.

The vendor handed the ice cream cone to the young boy. The hot sun warmed the ice cream cone within one minute. The ice cream cone dripped onto the bare fingers of the young boy. The young boy licked the ice cream cone very quickly.

Marek blew air into the red balloon at the party. The red balloon grew larger with every strong breath. The red balloon touched the sharp corner of the shelf. The red balloon burst with a loud sudden bang.

Helena wrote the long letter at the kitchen table. Helena carried the long letter to the village post office. The postal van took the long letter across the country. The grandmother read the long letter beside a warm fire.

The farmer buried the small seed in the dark soil. Warm rain soaked the dark soil for several quiet days. The green shoot pushed through the dark soil toward the light. The green shoot grew into a tall sunflower by August.

Lena rode the blue bicycle along the rough forest path. A sharp thorn pierced the front tyre near the valve. The front tyre lost all air within a few seconds. Lena patched the front tyre beside the forest path.

Petra leaned over the stone edge of the garden pond. The mobile phone slipped from the coat pocket of Petra. The mobile phone sank into the cold pond water. Petra fished the mobile phone out with a long stick.

The grey cat jumped onto the narrow hallway shelf. The grey cat pushed the blue vase toward the shelf edge. The blue vase fell onto the hard tiled floor. The owner swept the broken pieces into a paper bag.

Burnt toast filled the office kitchen with thick grey smoke. The fire alarm rang loudly through every floor of the building. The office workers walked calmly down the concrete emergency stairs. The fire officer switched off the fire alarm after ten minutes.

The children rolled three large snowballs across the white garden. The children stacked the three large snowballs into a snowman. Warm sunshine reached the snowman on a mild afternoon. The snowman melted into a wet patch on the grass.

A sharp rock scraped the bottom of the wooden rowing boat. Cold water seeped into the wooden rowing boat through a crack. The wooden rowing boat sat lower in the calm lake. Ivan rowed the wooden rowing boat quickly to the shore.

The cook tapped the brown egg against the metal pan. The brown egg cracked along one side of the shell. The contents of the brown egg slid into the hot pan. The cook sprinkled black pepper over the frying egg.

The leather wallet fell from the back trouser pocket of Omar. The leather wallet lay unnoticed on the busy pavement. The passing student spotted the leather wallet near a lamp post. The passing student returned the leather wallet to the police station.

The shopkeeper left the milk carton outside the cold storage room. Warm air surrounded the milk carton for the whole afternoon. The milk inside the milk carton turned sour and thick. The shopkeeper poured the sour milk down the kitchen drain.

Yusuf folded the white sheet into a sharp paper aeroplane. Yusuf threw the paper aeroplane across the quiet classroom. The paper aeroplane glided above three rows of wooden desks. The paper aeroplane landed on the open register of the teacher.

Agnes filled the copper kettle with cold tap water. Agnes placed the copper kettle onto the gas hob. The water inside the copper kettle reached a rolling boil. The copper kettle whistled across the whole quiet kitchen.

The student loaded heavy textbooks onto the thin wooden shelf. The thin wooden shelf bent slowly under the growing weight. The thin wooden shelf snapped in the middle with a crack. The heavy textbooks tumbled onto the bedroom carpet.

The shoelace on the left boot came loose during the walk. The runner stepped onto the shoelace at full speed. The runner tripped forward onto the muddy park path. The runner tied the shoelace with a double knot.

The boy hit the baseball hard toward the garden fence. The baseball flew high above the garden fence. The baseball struck the kitchen window of the neighbouring house. The kitchen window shattered across the tiled floor of the neighbour.

Ruth put the chocolate cake into the hot oven. Ruth fell asleep on the sofa for forty minutes. The chocolate cake turned black around the outer edges. Ruth threw the chocolate cake into the compost bin.
The bee landed on the picnic blanket beside the jam. Karl placed a bare hand onto the picnic blanket. The bee stung the palm of the hand of Karl. Karl pressed a cold cloth against the swollen palm.

Sofia dragged the old plank across the workshop bench. The wooden splinter entered the skin of the thumb of Sofia. The thumb turned red and sore within one hour. Sofia removed the wooden splinter with a pair of tweezers.

Heavy rain filled the deep puddle beside the bus stop. The delivery van drove fast through the deep puddle. Dirty water sprayed across the coat of the waiting passenger. The waiting passenger wiped the coat with a paper tissue.

Elias borrowed the thick history book from the town library. Elias read the thick history book over two quiet weeks. Elias carried the thick history book back to the town library. The librarian placed the thick history book onto the shelf.

The front tooth of the small girl became loose during breakfast. The small girl wiggled the front tooth with a clean finger. The front tooth came out during a bite of apple. The small girl placed the front tooth under a pillow.

The children built the tall sandcastle near the water line. The tide rose steadily along the wide sandy beach. A large wave reached the base of the tall sandcastle. The tall sandcastle collapsed into a low mound of sand.

Bo cast the fishing line into the deep green river. The large trout took the bait beneath a fallen branch. Bo pulled the large trout onto the muddy riverbank. Bo released the large trout back into the deep green river.

A sudden gust of wind swept along the stone harbour wall. The straw hat lifted from the head of Clara into the air. The straw hat rolled along the pier toward the cold water. Clara caught the straw hat at the very edge.

The old bulb flickered above the narrow kitchen table. The old bulb went dark during the evening meal. Janek fetched a stepladder from the hallway cupboard. Janek fitted a new bulb into the ceiling socket.

The company packed the ceramic plates into a cardboard box. The courier collected the cardboard box from the busy warehouse. The courier carried the cardboard box to a flat in Rome. The customer opened the cardboard box on the kitchen counter.

Amira chopped the ripe tomatoes with a very sharp knife. The sharp knife slipped across the wet chopping board. The sharp knife cut the fingertip of Amira near the nail. Amira wrapped a clean plaster around the bleeding fingertip.

Theo tuned the old guitar in the small rehearsal room. Theo turned the tuning peg far past the correct pitch. The top string snapped with a sharp metallic sound. Theo fitted a new string onto the old guitar.

The hikers switched on the torch inside the dark cave. The torch shone brightly across the wet limestone walls. The battery inside the torch died after twenty minutes. The hikers followed the cave wall back toward the daylight.

Heavy snow fell onto the driveway throughout the cold night. The snow reached the top of the garden steps by morning. Gustav shovelled the snow into a pile beside the hedge. The driveway lay clear and grey before the morning coffee.

The sparrow flew fast across the sunny back garden. The sparrow struck the clean glass of the patio door. The sparrow lay stunned on the warm stone patio. The sparrow recovered and flew into the hedge.

The toaster browned the thick slice of white bread. Ingrid placed the cold butter onto the hot toast. The cold butter melted into the surface of the hot toast. Ingrid ate the hot toast beside the kitchen window.

Nadia pointed the old camera toward the harbour at sunset. The old camera captured the orange light above the water. The photo shop printed the harbour picture on thick paper. Nadia hung the harbour picture in the narrow hallway.

Bruno pulled the zip of the winter coat too hard. The zip caught the thin lining of the winter coat. The zip broke apart near the bottom of the winter coat. Bruno took the winter coat to a small tailor shop.

A rusty nail lay flat on the supermarket car park. The car rolled slowly over the rusty nail. The rusty nail punctured the rear tyre of the car. The driver fitted the spare wheel beside the trolley bay.
Pilar set the pot of soup on a high flame. The soup bubbled hard against the rim of the pot. The soup boiled over onto the clean white hob. Pilar wiped the white hob with a damp cloth.

Hugo pushed the fountain pen into the shirt pocket. The cabin pressure changed during the long afternoon flight. The fountain pen leaked blue ink across the shirt pocket. Hugo soaked the shirt in cold water at the hotel.

The puppy found the leather shoe under the hallway bench. The puppy chewed the leather shoe for a quiet hour. The leather shoe lost most of the left heel. The owner hid the remaining shoes inside a tall cupboard.

The alarm clock rang at six on a dark morning. Ingmar switched off the alarm clock and returned to sleep. Ingmar woke at half past eight in bright daylight. Ingmar arrived at the office one hour late.

Roza boarded the evening train at the central station. Roza fell asleep against the cold carriage window. The evening train passed the correct stop without a sound. Roza left the evening train in a strange dark town.

Viktor searched a pocket for the house keys outside the door. The house keys slipped between two cold fingers. The house keys dropped through the metal drain grate. Viktor called a locksmith from the telephone of a neighbour.

Mai poured hot water over the green tea leaves. The green tea leaves opened slowly inside the glass pot. The water turned a pale gold colour within three minutes. Mai strained the green tea into a small cup.

Lars carried the jar of marbles down the wooden staircase. The jar of marbles slipped from a tired grip. The marbles scattered across every step of the wooden staircase. Lars collected the marbles one by one.

Diego lay on the beach without any sun cream. The strong sun burned the skin of the shoulders of Diego. The shoulders turned bright red by the early evening. Diego rubbed cool aloe gel onto the shoulders.

Rita hung the clean laundry on the line outside. A sudden storm arrived above the small back garden. The clean laundry soaked through within a few minutes. Rita carried the clean laundry back into the house.
The apple hung on a high branch through the warm summer. The apple ripened to a deep red in September. The apple fell from the high branch onto the grass. A hungry deer ate the apple before dawn.

A storm loosened three tiles on the old cottage roof. Rain entered the attic through the gap in the roof. Water dripped onto the wooden floor of the attic. The builder replaced the three tiles on a dry morning.
The child knocked the sugar bowl off the picnic table. The sugar spread across the warm paving stones. The ants found the sugar within one hour. The ants carried the sugar into a crack in the wall.

The child held the helium balloon by a thin string. The thin string slipped through the small fingers of the child. The helium balloon rose above the tall park trees. The helium balloon disappeared into the pale morning sky.

Sami stood the dominoes in a long curved line. Sami tapped the first domino with one finger. The first domino knocked the second domino onto the table. The whole line of dominoes fell within four seconds.
Warm rain fell on the front lawn for a week. The grass on the front lawn grew above the ankles. Frank pushed the lawnmower across the front lawn. The front lawn looked short and neat by evening.

The temperature dropped far below zero during the long night. The water inside the outdoor pipe turned to hard ice. The outdoor pipe cracked along a seam near the wall. The plumber replaced the outdoor pipe on a cold morning.

Nils carried the open tin of paint up the ladder. The open tin of paint tipped against the top rung. White paint poured onto the dark hallway floorboards. Nils scrubbed the hallway floorboards with a stiff brush.

Petra emptied the jigsaw pieces onto the wide dining table. Petra sorted the jigsaw pieces by colour and by edge. Petra joined the jigsaw pieces over three long evenings. The finished jigsaw showed a harbour under a grey sky.

Marta walked twenty kilometres in a pair of new boots. The new boots rubbed the skin of the left heel of Marta. A painful blister formed on the left heel. Marta covered the left heel with a soft dressing.

The children squeezed fresh lemons into a large glass jug. The children added cold water and three spoons of sugar. The children sold the lemonade beside the quiet village road. The large glass jug stood empty by four o'clock.

The cook left the cheese on the pantry shelf overnight. The smell of cheese spread through the dark pantry. The mouse entered the pantry through a small gap. The mouse ate the cheese down to the rind.

The crew planted the firework in the sand of the field. The crew lit the fuse of the firework at dusk. The firework rose high above the dark riverbank. The firework burst into green sparks above the water.

A heavy storm crossed the valley on a summer night. Lightning struck the tall pine on the ridge. The tall pine split from the crown to the roots. The forest ranger cleared the tall pine from the path.

A short earthquake shook the town at three in the morning. The shelves in the museum trembled for eleven seconds. The stone bust slid from the highest museum shelf. The stone bust cracked on the museum floor.

The children packed the snowball on the hill top. The children rolled the snowball down the long white slope. The snowball gathered snow along the whole white slope. The snowball stopped as a huge ball at the fence.
The sailors tied the boat with the old hemp rope. Salt water and sun weakened the old hemp rope. The old hemp rope frayed near the metal ring. The old hemp rope snapped during a strong evening tide.

Ella switched on the toy robot on the living room rug. The toy robot walked in circles for ten minutes. The batteries inside the toy robot lost all power. Ella fitted fresh batteries into the toy robot.

Otto placed the metal bucket under the garden tap. Otto opened the garden tap and walked into the shed. The water rose to the rim of the metal bucket. The water spilled across the stones around the metal bucket.

Hana hammered the small nail into the bedroom wall. Hana hung the framed photograph on the small nail. The small nail slipped out of the soft plaster. The framed photograph fell onto the wooden floor.

Cold weather froze the village pond during January. The skater stepped onto the thin ice near the reeds. The thin ice cracked under the weight of the skater. The skater crawled back to the bank on both elbows.

The office bought the coffee machine for the small kitchen. Limescale built up inside the coffee machine over two years. The coffee machine stopped heating the water in March. The office ordered a new coffee machine from a supplier.

The waiter filled the tall glasses with cold white wine. The guests raised the tall glasses above the long table. Two tall glasses struck each other with great force. One tall glass cracked along the thin rim.

Ana tied the rowing boat to the wooden jetty. The knot on the rowing boat loosened during the night. The rowing boat drifted into the middle of the lake. A fisherman towed the rowing boat back to the jetty.

Kai placed the popcorn bag inside the small microwave. The kernels inside the popcorn bag heated for two minutes. The kernels burst against the walls of the popcorn bag. Kai opened the popcorn bag and released hot steam.

A dry wind lifted the fine sand across the wide beach. The fine sand blew into the left eye of the swimmer. The left eye of the swimmer watered and turned red. The swimmer rinsed the left eye with clean water.

The hen sat on the speckled egg for three weeks. A small crack appeared across the shell of the speckled egg. The chick pushed through the shell of the speckled egg. The chick dried into a ball of yellow down.

The caterpillar ate the leaves of the nettle for two weeks. The caterpillar spun a hard case around the whole body. The hard case hung from a nettle stem through the winter. A red butterfly climbed out of the hard case in spring.

Frost covered the kitchen window during a still cold night. The morning sun reached the kitchen window at eight. The frost melted into small drops on the glass. The small drops ran down to the wooden sill.

Selma left the loaf of bread in a warm damp cupboard. Green mould appeared on the crust of the loaf of bread. The green mould spread across the whole loaf of bread. Selma threw the loaf of bread into the compost.

The bottle of ink stood near the edge of the desk. The tail of the cat brushed the bottle of ink. The bottle of ink tipped over onto the open notebook. The blue ink soaked through six pages of the open notebook.

The morning train left the station twenty minutes late. The morning train stopped outside the junction for signal repairs. The morning train reached the city at ten o'clock. Ravi missed the connecting coach to the coast.

Lucia paddled the surfboard beyond the line of breaking waves. A tall wave rose behind the surfboard without warning. The tall wave knocked Lucia off the surfboard. Lucia surfaced beside the surfboard and coughed hard.

The children played football in the narrow side street. The football bounced off the wall of a corner house. The football rolled under a parked delivery van. The driver of the delivery van pushed the football back.

Jonas held the mobile phone above the concrete stairs. The mobile phone slipped and struck the bottom step. The screen of the mobile phone cracked in three lines. Jonas taped the screen and booked a repair.

The hedge along the driveway grew above the garden wall. The hedge blocked the light from the front windows. Emil trimmed the hedge with a pair of long shears. The front windows caught the afternoon sun.

Sara pulled the front door shut behind a heavy suitcase. The house keys lay on the table inside the hallway. Sara searched every coat pocket on the cold doorstep. A neighbour opened the front door with a spare key.

Tomas put the chocolate bar into a trouser pocket. The chocolate bar warmed against the leg during a long walk. The chocolate bar melted inside the paper wrapper. Tomas washed the trousers in cold water at home.

Rain fell on the bicycle behind the garden shed. Rust formed along the whole length of the bicycle chain. The bicycle chain stiffened and squeaked on every turn. Bram cleaned the bicycle chain with oil and a rag.

Yasmin threw the flat stone into the still pond. The flat stone broke the surface with a small splash. Rings of water spread outward across the still pond. The rings of water faded at the reeds along the bank.

Meera mixed the butter and the sugar in a bowl. Meera shaped the dough into twelve round biscuits. The twelve round biscuits baked in the oven for ten minutes. The twelve round biscuits cooled on a wire rack.

The waiter stacked the plates high on the metal tray. The waiter caught a chair leg near the kitchen door. The metal tray tipped forward onto the tiled floor. The plates smashed into pieces across the tiled floor.

Heavy rain fell on the hills above the village for days. The river rose above the banks near the stone bridge. The water covered the low field beside the stone bridge. The farmer moved the cattle to a higher field.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.

Lisbon served as the capital of Portugal beside the Tagus River. Ottawa served as the capital of Canada beside the Ottawa River. Nairobi served as the capital of Kenya in East Africa. Santiago served as the capital of Chile below the Andes.

The polar bear hunted seals across the ice of the Arctic. The camel crossed the sand dunes of the Sahara Desert. The penguin nested on the rocky coasts of Antarctica. The jaguar hunted along the rivers of the Amazon rainforest.

Mars showed a red surface covered with fine iron dust. Jupiter carried a great storm within the thick cloud bands. Venus held a dense atmosphere of hot carbon dioxide. Saturn wore a bright system of ice and rock rings.

Beethoven composed nine symphonies during a long career in Vienna. Bach worked as a church musician in the city of Leipzig. Mozart wrote the opera The Magic Flute for a Vienna theatre. Haydn served the Esterhazy family at a Hungarian palace.

Rembrandt painted many self portraits in the city of Amsterdam. Frida Kahlo painted vivid personal scenes in Mexico City. Turner painted storms and light along the English coast. Hokusai produced woodblock prints of Mount Fuji in Japan.

Marie Curie discovered radium in a laboratory in Paris. Alexander Fleming noticed the mould behind penicillin in London. Gregor Mendel studied pea plants in a monastery garden. Dmitri Mendeleev arranged the chemical elements into a periodic table.

The Nile flowed north through Egypt into the Mediterranean Sea. The Amazon carried more water than any other river on Earth. The Danube passed through several capitals of central Europe. The Mekong flowed south through the countries of Southeast Asia.

Mount Kilimanjaro rose above the plains of northern Tanzania. Mount Fuji stood as a broad cone west of Tokyo. The Matterhorn marked the border between Switzerland and Italy. Denali rose above the wide interior of Alaska.

Portuguese served as the main language of Brazil for centuries. Finnish belonged to a family apart from the Germanic languages. Swahili served as a common language across much of East Africa. Quechua survived in the highlands of Peru and Bolivia.

The yen circulated as the official currency of Japan. The rupee circulated as the official currency of India. The forint circulated as the official currency of Hungary. The real circulated as the official currency of Brazil.

Tennis required a stringed racket and a felt covered ball. Fencing required a thin blade and a wire mesh mask. Archery required a curved bow and a set of arrows. Curling required a heavy granite stone and a stiff brush.

Gutenberg built a printing press in the city of Mainz. Alexander Graham Bell patented an early telephone in the United States. Ada Lovelace wrote notes on a mechanical calculating engine. Thomas Edison developed a practical light bulb in New Jersey.

The Maya built stone cities in the forests of Central America. The Egyptians raised stone pyramids beside the western bank of the Nile. The Romans laid paved roads across a very wide empire. The Inca built terraced fields high in the Andes.

The oak produced acorns in the woodlands of northern Europe. The birch carried thin white bark across cold northern forests. The baobab stored water inside a thick swollen trunk in Africa. The maple turned deep red in the autumn of Canada.

The albatross glided for hours above the Southern Ocean. The woodpecker drilled holes into the trunks of dead trees. The kiwi lived on the forest floor of New Zealand. The flamingo took a pink colour from a diet of shrimp.

The salmon returned to the rivers of birth for spawning. The eel travelled from Europe to the Sargasso Sea. The pufferfish carried a strong toxin inside the body. The seahorse held eggs in a pouch on the male.

The dragonfly hunted small insects above ponds and slow rivers. The ant carried loads far heavier than the body weight. The cicada spent many years underground before a short summer. The firefly produced cold light in the warm evening air.

Copper carried electricity through the wires of many old houses. Mercury remained liquid at ordinary room temperature. Titanium resisted corrosion inside salt water for long periods. Lead blocked much of the radiation from medical machines.

The ruby took a deep red colour from traces of chromium. The emerald came from mines in the mountains of Colombia. The opal showed shifting colours inside a soft milky stone. The diamond scratched almost every other natural material.

Hail formed inside the strong updraughts of a summer thunderstorm. A rainbow appeared when sunlight passed through a curtain of rain. Frost formed on clear nights with almost no wind. Fog settled over the river valley in calm autumn mornings.

Chess used sixty four squares and sixteen pieces for each player. Go used black and white stones on a nineteen line grid. Backgammon used dice and twenty four narrow triangles. Mancala used small seeds and a row of shallow pits.

Roquefort ripened in limestone caves in the south of France. Parmesan aged for years in the region around Parma. Gouda took a name from a market town in the Netherlands. Halloumi resisted melting on a hot grill in Cyprus.

Baguette came from French bakeries as a long thin crusty loaf. Naan baked against the hot wall of a clay oven. Pumpernickel baked slowly for many hours in Westphalia. Injera carried a sour taste from a fermented teff batter.

The banana grew on tall plants in the tropics of Asia. The kiwi fruit spread from China to orchards in New Zealand. The pineapple came from the lowlands of South America. The fig grew around the Mediterranean for thousands of years.

The potato came from the high fields of the Andes. The carrot took an orange colour from a pigment called carotene. The cabbage grew well in the cool climate of northern Europe. The pumpkin spread from the Americas into gardens worldwide.

Saffron came from the dried threads inside a purple crocus. Cinnamon came from the inner bark of a tropical tree. Black pepper grew as a climbing vine in southern India. Vanilla came from the cured pods of a climbing orchid.

Green tea came from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant. Coffee came from the roasted seeds of a red cherry. Cider came from the pressed juice of ripe apples. Mate came from the dried leaves of a South American holly.

The Sahara covered a wide band across the north of Africa. The Atacama received almost no rain for many long years. The Gobi stretched across parts of Mongolia and China. The Namib met the cold water of the Atlantic Ocean.

Madagascar held plants and animals found nowhere else on Earth. Iceland sat above a ridge between two moving plates. Sicily lay in the middle of the Mediterranean Sea. Borneo carried dense rainforest across three separate countries.

The Golden Gate Bridge crossed a strait beside San Francisco. The Ponte Vecchio carried small shops across the Arno in Florence. Tower Bridge opened in two halves above the Thames. The Charles Bridge crossed the Vltava in the city of Prague.

The Eiffel Tower rose in iron lattice above the city of Paris. The Leaning Tower of Pisa tilted on soft ground in Tuscany. The Burj Khalifa stood above the desert city of Dubai. The Space Needle marked the skyline of the city of Seattle.

The Louvre held a vast art collection beside the River Seine. The Prado gathered Spanish painting in the centre of Madrid. The Hermitage occupied a former winter palace in Saint Petersburg. The Uffizi displayed Renaissance work in the city of Florence.

Jane Austen wrote novels about English country society. Leo Tolstoy wrote very long novels in the Russian countryside. Chinua Achebe wrote about village life in colonial Nigeria. Gabriel Garcia Marquez wrote about an imagined Colombian town.

Emily Dickinson wrote hundreds of short poems in Amherst. Pablo Neruda wrote love poems in the Spanish language. Basho wrote brief travel poems along the roads of Japan. Rumi wrote mystical verse in the Persian language.

Shakespeare wrote plays for a theatre beside the River Thames. Chekhov wrote plays about quiet unhappiness in rural Russia. Ibsen wrote plays about family secrets in Norway. Aeschylus wrote tragedies for the festivals of ancient Athens.

Socrates questioned the citizens of Athens in public places. Confucius taught rules of proper conduct in ancient China. Immanuel Kant kept a very regular routine in Konigsberg. Hypatia taught mathematics and philosophy in ancient Alexandria.

Euclid organised geometry into a set of clear ordered books. Emmy Noether linked symmetry to conservation laws in physics. Srinivasa Ramanujan produced remarkable results with little formal training. Alan Turing described an abstract machine for computation.

Galileo observed the moons of Jupiter through an early telescope. Edwin Hubble measured the distance to faraway galaxies. Caroline Herschel discovered several comets from an English garden. Tycho Brahe recorded precise star positions without any telescope.

Zheng He sailed large fleets across the Indian Ocean. Roald Amundsen reached the South Pole with a team of dogs. Ibn Battuta travelled across much of the medieval Muslim world. Marco Polo described a long journey to the court of China.

Queen Victoria reigned over Britain for more than sixty years. Louis the Fourteenth built a vast palace at Versailles. Mansa Musa ruled a wealthy empire in medieval Mali. Ashoka ruled a large Indian empire and promoted Buddhism.

Neuschwanstein rose on a rocky hill in southern Bavaria. Edinburgh Castle sat above the old town on volcanic rock. Himeji Castle stood as a white complex in western Japan. The Alhambra crowned a hill above the city of Granada.

Notre Dame stood on an island in the middle of Paris. The Sagrada Familia rose slowly above the streets of Barcelona. Cologne Cathedral raised twin towers beside the River Rhine. Saint Basil's Cathedral showed bright twisted domes on Red Square.

Angkor Wat spread across a very wide site in northern Cambodia. Borobudur rose in stone terraces on the island of Java. The Parthenon stood on the rocky hill above ancient Athens. Todaiji housed a giant bronze Buddha in the city of Nara.

Tango developed in the port districts of Buenos Aires. Flamenco combined guitar and voice and footwork in Andalusia. Kathak told long stories through gesture in northern India. Samba filled the streets of Rio during carnival.

Diwali filled homes with small oil lamps across much of India. Holi covered crowds in bright coloured powder each spring. Songkran turned into a water festival across Thailand. Obon welcomed ancestors with lanterns and dances across Japan.

Jazz grew from the mixed musical traditions of New Orleans. Reggae developed in the recording studios of Kingston. Fado carried a mood of longing through the streets of Lisbon. Highlife blended bright horns and guitars in Ghana.

La Scala presented opera in the centre of the city of Milan. The Sydney Opera House showed white shells beside a wide harbour. The Palais Garnier displayed heavy ornament in central Paris. The Teatro Colon offered fine acoustics in Buenos Aires.

Swan Lake used music by Tchaikovsky and a story of a swan queen. Giselle told a story of a village girl and a ghostly forest. The Rite of Spring caused an uproar at a Paris premiere. La Bayadere set a romantic story in an imagined India.

Bunraku used large puppets and three visible operators in Japan. Wayang kulit cast shadow puppets against a lit cloth screen in Java. Punch and Judy played inside small seaside booths in England. Karagoz used flat shadow figures in Turkish coffee houses.

Triceratops carried three horns and a wide bony neck frill. Velociraptor grew to roughly the size of a large turkey. Stegosaurus carried tall bony plates along the whole back. Brachiosaurus reached leaves high in ancient conifer trees.

The woolly mammoth carried long curved tusks across cold steppes. The sabre toothed cat used very long upper canine teeth. The giant ground sloth reached the size of a small elephant. The woolly rhinoceros grazed the cold plains of ice age Europe.

The chameleon moved each eye in a separate direction. The Komodo dragon hunted large prey on a few Indonesian islands. The gecko climbed smooth glass with tiny hairs on the toes. The tortoise carried a heavy shell of fused bone.

The axolotl kept feathery gills through the whole adult life. The poison dart frog carried strong toxins in the skin. The caecilian burrowed through soft soil without any legs. The fire salamander showed bright yellow marks on black skin.

The blue whale fed on tiny krill in cold ocean water. The dolphin found prey with rapid pulses of sound. The walrus used long tusks to climb onto the ice. The sea otter cracked shells with a small flat stone.

The lion lived in social groups on the African savannah. The snow leopard crossed steep ground in the high Himalaya. The cheetah reached great speed over very short distances. The tiger swam well in the wide rivers of Asia.

The panda ate bamboo in the mountain forests of China. The polar bear crossed sea ice in search of seals. The sloth bear ate termites in the forests of India. The spectacled bear lived in the cloud forests of the Andes.

The gorilla ate leaves and stems in central African forests. The orangutan moved through the canopy of Borneo and Sumatra. The lemur lived only on the island of Madagascar. The tarsier hunted insects at night with enormous eyes.

The beaver felled small trees with strong front teeth. The capybara grazed beside rivers across South America. The naked mole rat lived in long tunnels in East Africa. The porcupine carried sharp loose quills across the back.

The goat climbed steep rocks with remarkable balance. The duck kept feathers dry with a light oily coating. The donkey carried loads along mountain paths for centuries. The goose guarded farmyards with a loud harsh call.

The dachshund hunted badgers inside narrow underground burrows. The border collie herded sheep across the hills of Scotland. The saluki ran down fast game in desert regions. The Newfoundland pulled people from cold rough water.

The Arabian carried a distinctive dished face and a high tail. The Shire pulled heavy loads on English farms. The Icelandic horse kept an extra gait called the tolt. The Appaloosa showed spotted patterns across the coat.

The Highland cow carried a long shaggy coat in Scotland. The Holstein produced very large quantities of milk. The Zebu carried a shoulder hump and tolerated hot climates. The Hereford grew a white face above a red body.

The Merino produced very fine soft wool in Australia. Cashmere came from the undercoat of certain mountain goats. Mohair came from the fleece of the Angora goat. Tweed came from wool woven in the Scottish islands.

The honeybee stored nectar as honey inside wax combs. The bumblebee flew in cooler weather than most other bees. The mason bee sealed nests inside hollow stems with mud. The stingless bee produced honey in tropical forests.

The monarch travelled thousands of kilometres to Mexican forests. The swallowtail carried long tails on the hind wings. The blue morpho flashed metallic blue in Amazonian light. The peacock butterfly showed large eyespots across the wings.

The tarantula lived for many years in warm dry burrows. The jumping spider used sharp vision to stalk prey. The orb weaver rebuilt a round web almost every night. The diving bell spider carried air underwater inside silk.

The chanterelle grew golden and funnel shaped in old woodland. The fly agaric carried white spots on a bright red cap. The truffle grew underground near the roots of oaks. The shiitake grew on hardwood logs in East Asia.

The bracken spread across hillsides through creeping underground stems. Sphagnum moss stored huge amounts of water in peat bogs. The tree fern grew a tall trunk in New Zealand forests. The maidenhair fern grew beside damp limestone walls.

The tulip drew enormous prices in the Dutch Republic. The lotus rose above muddy water on a long straight stem. The orchid family included many thousands of separate species. The sunflower turned the young flower head toward the sun.

The saguaro grew tall branching arms in the Sonoran Desert. The prickly pear produced edible pads and sweet red fruit. The barrel cactus stored water inside a thick ribbed body. The Christmas cactus flowered during the short days of winter.

Rice grew in flooded paddy fields across much of Asia. Quinoa grew at high altitude in the Andes. Millet tolerated dry soils across the African Sahel. Barley grew well in the short summers of the far north.

Basil grew best in warm soil under bright sunlight. Rosemary kept needle leaves through the Mediterranean winter. Mint spread quickly through gardens by underground runners. Thyme grew low across dry stony ground.
The cashew grew below a fleshy false fruit. The walnut ripened inside a thick green husk. The pistachio split the hard shell during ripening. The macadamia came from forests in eastern Australia.

Olive oil came from the pressed fruit of the olive tree. Sesame oil came from small seeds toasted before pressing. Coconut oil came from the dried flesh of the coconut. Rapeseed oil came from the seeds of a yellow flowered crop.

Vitamin C prevented scurvy among sailors on long voyages. Vitamin D formed inside human skin under sunlight. Vitamin A supported vision in very dim light. Vitamin B twelve came almost entirely from animal foods.

The liver performed hundreds of chemical tasks in the body. The kidney filtered waste products from the blood. The pancreas released digestive enzymes into the small intestine. The spleen removed old red blood cells.

The stapes sat inside the middle ear as the smallest bone. The skull protected the brain with a set of fused plates. The femur formed the longest bone in the human body. The ribs formed a curved cage around the heart and lungs.

The retina held cells for colour and for very dim light. The cochlea turned sound waves into nerve signals. The tongue carried receptors for salt and sweet and bitter. The nose detected thousands of separate odour molecules.

Red blood cells carried oxygen with a protein called haemoglobin. White blood cells attacked bacteria and other invaders. Platelets formed clots at the sites of small wounds. Plasma carried nutrients and hormones through the vessels.

Helium remained a gas at extremely low temperatures. Carbon formed millions of different chemical compounds. Gold resisted almost every common chemical reaction. Iron formed most of the core of the planet Earth.

Nitrogen made up most of the air in the atmosphere. Ozone absorbed ultraviolet light high above the ground. Methane rose from wetlands and from flooded rice fields. Argon filled some older light bulbs as an inert gas.

Citric acid gave lemons a sharp sour taste. Acetic acid gave vinegar a distinctive strong smell. Hydrochloric acid supported digestion inside the human stomach. Formic acid appeared in the sting of certain ants.

Quartz formed six sided crystals in many kinds of rock. Halite formed neat cubic crystals in dried salt lakes. Pyrite formed golden cubes and earned the name fool's gold. Mica split into very thin transparent sheets.

Granite formed from magma cooling slowly deep underground. Limestone formed from the shells of countless sea creatures. Slate split into flat sheets under great pressure. Basalt formed from lava cooling quickly at the surface.

Mauna Loa built a broad gentle shield in Hawaii. Vesuvius buried Roman towns under ash and rock. Eyjafjallajokull sent ash clouds across European airspace. Krakatoa produced an explosion heard across enormous distances.

The San Andreas Fault marked a plate boundary through California. The Ring of Fire circled the edges of the Pacific Ocean. The Richter scale measured the size of earthquakes. Tsunamis followed some undersea earthquakes across whole oceans.

Glaciers carved wide valleys with steep bare rock walls. Moraines formed from rock and gravel left by melting ice. Crevasses opened where a glacier crossed very uneven ground. Icebergs broke from glaciers that reached the open sea.

Stalactites hung from cave ceilings as slow growing stone spikes. Stalagmites rose from cave floors below dripping water. The Mammoth Cave system stretched for hundreds of kilometres. Lascaux held painted animals on the walls in France.

Cumulus clouds formed puffy heaps on warm summer days. Cirrus clouds formed thin ice streaks very high above. Stratus clouds spread a flat grey sheet across the sky. Lenticular clouds formed smooth lens shapes above mountains.

Betelgeuse glowed red in the shoulder of the constellation Orion. Sirius shone as the brightest star in the night sky. Polaris sat almost above the North Pole of Earth. Proxima Centauri lay closest to the Sun among all stars.
Europa hid a salty ocean beneath a thick shell of ice. Titan carried lakes of liquid methane across the surface. Io held hundreds of active volcanoes. Phobos circled Mars in less than eight hours.

Halley's Comet returned near the Sun about every seventy six years. Ceres counted as the largest object in the asteroid belt. The Kuiper Belt held icy bodies beyond the orbit of Neptune. Meteor showers appeared when Earth crossed old comet trails.

The Milky Way held a supermassive black hole at the centre. Andromeda approached the Milky Way at considerable speed. The Magellanic Clouds appeared to observers in the southern sky. Elliptical galaxies contained mostly old red stars.

The Hubble Space Telescope orbited above the atmosphere of Earth. The Very Large Array spread radio dishes across a New Mexico plain. Jodrell Bank tracked early spacecraft from a field in England. The Keck telescopes stood near the summit of Mauna Kea.

The dhow carried triangular sails across the Indian Ocean. The junk used battened sails in the seas around China. The longship carried Norse crews along rivers and coasts. The clipper raced tea cargoes across long ocean routes.

The Trans Siberian Railway crossed Russia over many time zones. The London Underground opened the first city metro line. The Shinkansen carried passengers at high speed across Japan. The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway climbed steep hills on narrow tracks.

The Concorde carried passengers faster than the speed of sound. The Spruce Goose flew only once above a California harbour. The Zeppelin carried passengers under a rigid gas filled frame. The Harrier took off from remarkably short runways.

The Suez Canal linked the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea. The Panama Canal cut across a narrow neck of Central America. The Grand Canal ran a great distance across eastern China. The Erie Canal linked the Hudson River to the Great Lakes.

The Appian Way carried Roman traffic south from the city of Rome. The Silk Road linked China to the markets of the Mediterranean. The Inca road network crossed the Andes over thousands of kilometres. Route Sixty Six crossed the United States from Chicago to California.
